title: Scientists Find New Kind of Friction with Magnets
summary: Scientists at the University of Konstanz have discovered a new type of friction that happens without touching. Friction is the force that makes it hard to slide things, like when you push a heavy box. Usually, friction gets stronger when you press things together harder, which is called Amontons' law. But in this experiment, they used magnets that never touch each other. They found that friction can reach a high peak when the magnets are at a certain distance apart. This happens because the magnets have competing preferences for how to line up, causing them to switch back and forth as they move, which creates energy loss and friction. This shows that friction isn't always about surfaces rubbing; it can come from how magnets behave inside a system.

keywords.explanation: a force that makes it hard to slide or move things
objects that can attract or repel each other without touching
an old rule that says friction increases when you press things together harder
how far apart things are from each other
when energy is used up or wasted during movement

options: A new type of friction with magnets
A way to make magnets stronger

background_read: Friction is a common force we experience every day, like when you rub your hands together to warm them up or when you try to push a heavy object across the floor. It usually happens because two surfaces are touching and rubbing against each other, which makes it harder to move. Scientists have long studied friction and made rules about it, such as Amontons' law, which says that the harder you press two things together, the more friction there is. In this article, researchers found something new: friction can happen even without any touching, using magnets that interact from a distance. This discovery helps us understand that friction isn't always about surfaces; it can come from how things like magnets behave inside a system, which is exciting for science and might lead to new technologies.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists at the University of Konstanz
WHAT: Discovered a new type of friction without touching, using magnets
WHY: To show that friction can come from magnetic behavior, not just surface contact, breaking an old law called Amontons' law

perspectives.description: This shows how science can find new things that challenge old ideas, helping us learn more about the world.
